Spec-driven development fails for the same reason every documentation-first initiative fails. It asks humans to do continuous maintenance work that nobody sees and nobody rewards. 

The fix is making the spec bidirectional: both humans and agents read from it and write to it. https://t.co/TfN7H2J8aV

/loop is a powerful new way to schedule recurring tasks, for up to 3 days at a time

eg. “/loop babysit all my PRs. Auto-fix build issues and when comments come in, use a worktree agent to fix them”

Another realization I only voiced in this pod:

**This is the year of the Subagent**

- every practical multiagent problem is a subagent problem 
- agents are being RLed to control other agents (Cursor, Kimi, Claude, Cognition)

nanochat now trains GPT-2 capability model in just 2 hours on a single 8XH100 node (down from ~3 hours 1 month ago). Getting a lot closer to ~interactive! A bunch of tuning and features (fp8) went in but the biggest difference was a switch of the dataset from FineWeb-edu to https://t.co/qragh6hJL6

Visualize Agent Teams workflow in Claude Code

npx claude-code-templates@latest --teams

skills solved the agent knowledge problem at the wrong level of the stack and im hoping that it's not too late to undo that damage over time

for 90%+ of skills, they are basically your existing docs with a small tweak to tell an agent how to do the operation rather than a human

Introducing https://t.co/eMEnAihZZL, a continual learning layer that works across all your coding agents, built on top of @pydanti_ai and @DSPyOSS.

You debug something in @claudeai Code. You discover a pattern in @cursor_ai. You make an architectural decision in Codex. Tomorrow? https://t.co/T4lhvzGR6G

Most teams can build AI agents.
Very few can operate them continuously.

Bottlenecks I keep seeing: context routing, failure recovery, cost guardrails, clean handoffs.

Mission Control is one layer.
Next to it, I'm exploring deeper layers: 

LACP (local agent control) + OneClaw https://t.co/5uVyNYXMeB

- bash pre-check, zero tokens when idle
- webhook fires opus only when needed.

Features:
→ Mobile-first + cross-platform
→ Run multiple agents in parallel across repositories
→ Launch agent-based requests from issues and PRs
→ Swipe actions for close, merge, create PR, https://t.co/LWUyrVdONW

Since open-sourcing Mission Control, one thing became obvious:
people don’t struggle with “running agents” — they struggle with operating them.

The hard parts are:

• visibility across agent runs
• failure recovery / reconnect behavior
• cost + token guardrails
• task flow

Readout is a fully native macOS app I’ve been building for myself. It provides a real-time overview of your dev environment and Claude Code config. All local, no account required. It's still very much a beta, but now available to try: https://t.co/gpKj1KCpcM https://t.co/R14grcG8NE

Just pushed a cool update to Readout: session replays. Pick any past Claude Code session and scrub through the full timeline. Every prompt, tool call, file change. Files light up as edits land. Play back at different speeds or step through manually. 

→ https://t.co/gpKj1KCpcM https://t.co/yQRFblmiqm
